metadata.title: Consumer and auto ABS and subprime - the household-credit tail, and the collateral-fraud failure mode (Tricolor, First Brands)
metadata.frame: Total US household debt hit ~$18.8T (Q1 2026) with a benign 4.8% aggregate delinquency that masks a stressed subprime tail: subprime auto 60+ day delinquency at a 32-year high (6.74%), repossessions at 2009 levels, student-loan delinquency re-priced from ~0 to 7.7%+, and ~$400B of invisible BNPL 'phantom debt.' Tricolor and First Brands (both 2025) showed the failure mode is collateral fraud in the securitization/factoring funding chain, not just borrower default. Companion to [[macro-uncovered-risk-pools]] and [[macro-cre-privatecredit]].
metadata.relevance: Adds the household-credit tail to the uncovered stack; the Tricolor/First Brands double-pledge cases tie the subprime tail to bank warehouse lines and private-credit/ABS opacity (the self-marked, under-verified funding chain).
metadata.sources: NY Fed Household Debt & Credit (Q1 2026, released May 2026); DOJ-SDNY (Tricolor + First Brands charges); CNBC (JPMorgan writedown); Fitch Auto ABS index; Cox Automotive (repossessions); Affirm/Klarna filings; CFPB BNPL market report; GlobalCapital/Asset Securitization Report (ABS issuance). Full URLs in the rendered .md.

collateral_fraud_cases.summary: Tricolor and First Brands: same pattern weeks apart - collateral double-pledged across financiers.

points.detail: Subprime auto lender (to undocumented/thin-file borrowers) filed Chapter 7 ~Sept 2025 amid 'systemic levels of fraud.' DOJ (Dec 2025) charged the CEO/COO with running a 'continuing financial crimes enterprise,' allegedly double-pledging collateral (the trustee's forensic review identified ~$548M double-pledged across ~31,000 loans; the broader alleged fraud is larger) and using duplicate VINs and falsified loan tapes to raise billions. JPMorgan took a ~$170M writedown (Q3 2025); Fifth Third flagged ~$200M potential; Barclays also exposed.

Auto-parts supplier (Chapter 11, Sept 28, 2025): ~$5B sales but >$9B liabilities and $12M cash. ~$3.0B of invoices sold to factors, ~$2.5B unmatched to real sales (fabricated/double-pledged receivables). DOJ-SDNY charges; ex-CFO pleaded guilty (Mar 2026); Jefferies' Point Bonita flagged ~$715M at risk.

The subprime-auto-ABS and trade-finance/factoring chains relied on lender trust in self-reported loan tapes/invoices; the SAME collateral pledged across multiple warehouse lines/factors went undetected - a collateral-verification failure at the FUNDING layer, the same trust-the-self-report defect this project tracks in marks.

auto_stress.summary: Subprime auto is the canary - delinquency at a 32-year high, repos at 2009 levels.

points.detail: Total US auto debt ~$1.69T (Q1 2026, NY Fed). Subprime auto 60+ day delinquency 6.74% (Dec 2025, Fitch) - a 32-year series high. Auto flow into serious (90+) delinquency ~2.94% (Q1 2025), steady into 2026.
~1.73M vehicles repossessed in 2024 (Cox) - highest since 2009, +16% YoY; 2025 projected >3M (a projection, graded weak). Auto default rate 3.13% (2024), highest since 2011.

cards_bnpl_student.summary: A K-shaped consumer: prime current, subprime/young/BNPL stressed.

points.detail: Credit-card balances ~$1.25T (Q1 2026); flow into serious delinquency ~7.04% (Q1 2025), early-delinquency transition ~8.6% (Q1 2026) - elevated but plateauing; stress concentrated in subprime/lower-income.

BNPL US GMV ~$122B (2025); outstanding balances estimated >$400B are largely INVISIBLE to credit bureaus, understating true household leverage. 47% of BNPL users reported a late payment in the past year (LendingTree, Mar 2026, up from 41%); ~8% have used BNPL for groceries (DoorDash/Klarna). Affirm 30+ day delinquency ~2.8%; Klarna trades below its Sept-2025 IPO price with rising credit losses.

~$1.66T total; the pandemic on-ramp ended Sept 2024 and servicers resumed bureau reporting Feb 2025 - 90+ day delinquency jumped to ~7.74% of student debt (Q1 2025) from <1%, with >2.2M borrowers seeing scores fall >100 points; the national average score fell ~717->715, the sharpest one-year drop since the Great Recession.

abs_chain.summary: Record ABS issuance disperses the risk off bank balance sheets - and obscures collateral quality.

points.detail: Total US ABS ~$358B YTD 2025 (+16.8%, auto-driven; record); auto ABS ~$127B; unsecured consumer-loan ABS set records. Subprime auto + unsecured consumer credit is originated, warehoused by banks (JPMorgan, Fifth Third, Barclays), then securitized off balance sheet into ABS bought by asset managers/insurers reaching for yield - banks retain warehouse-line tail risk (the Tricolor channel).

synthesis.summary: A benign aggregate over a stressed, opaque tail.

points.detail: Household debt is at a record $18.8T with a placid 4.8% aggregate delinquency - but the tail is at multi-decade highs (subprime auto 6.74%, student 7.7%+, BNPL late incidence 47%) and ~$400B of leverage is invisible. Tricolor/First Brands show the acute failure mode is collateral fraud in the funding chain - the same 'trust the self-reported number' defect that runs through the bank HTM, private-credit NAV, and pension-mark threads. The off-balance-sheet ABS chain disperses the loss beyond the originating bank while hiding collateral quality.

non_claims: No claim of system-wide household-credit insolvency - aggregate delinquency is benign; the point is a stressed, partly-invisible subprime tail plus a collateral-fraud failure mode.
BNPL '~$400B phantom debt' and '47% late' rest on survey/secondary sources (graded contested); the >3M-2025-repossession figure is a projection (weak).
Tricolor's double-pledge (~$548M per the trustee's review) is distinct from JPMorgan's ~$170M writedown - not conflated; Fifth Third's ~$200M is potential, not finalized.
Overlay edges are EXCLUDED from the SCC / Z3 / TLA+ proofs.

edges.raw_instrument: auto ABS / warehouse financing (~$1.69T auto balances; subprime 60+ delinquency 6.74% Dec 2025, a 32-yr high; repos at 2009 levels)
edges.note: OVERLAY: household-credit tail; excluded from proofs

edges.raw_instrument: warehouse financing against double-pledged auto collateral (~$548M per trustee; ~31,000 loans); JPMorgan ~$170M writedown, Fifth Third ~$200M, Barclays - collateral-fraud failure in the funding chain
edges.note: OVERLAY: collateral-fraud channel; excluded from proofs

edges.raw_instrument: trade-finance/factoring receivables (~$2.5B fabricated invoices, multi-factor double-pledge) - factoring/private-lending opacity, the Tricolor pattern in trade finance
edges.note: OVERLAY: factoring fraud; excluded from proofs

edges.raw_instrument: unreported installment financing (~$400B est., largely invisible to bureaus) - understates true household leverage; 47% of users reported a late payment (2026)
edges.note: OVERLAY: invisible leverage; excluded from proofs

limits: Documents the household-credit tail: total debt ~$18.8T (Q1 2026), aggregate delinquency 4.8% over a stressed tail - subprime auto 6.74% (32-yr high), repos ~1.73M (2024, 2009 levels), cards ~$1.25T, student ~$1.66T with 90+ delinquency ~7.74% (Q1 2025), BNPL ~$122B GMV / ~$400B invisible / 47% late; the Tricolor (~$548M double-pledged per trustee, JPMorgan ~$170M) and First Brands (~$2.5B fabricated invoices) collateral-fraud cases; and record ABS issuance (~$358B 2025) dispersing risk off-balance-sheet. Overlay edges excluded from proofs; several figures contested/weak.

checks.result: Tricolor Ch.7 ~Sept 2025, DOJ Dec 2025, ~$548M double-pledged (trustee), JPMorgan ~$170M; First Brands Ch.11 Sept 28 2025, ~$2.5B fabricated invoices, CFO guilty Mar 2026.
Auto debt ~$1.69T (Q1 2026); subprime 60+ 6.74% (Dec 2025, Fitch, 32-yr high); ~1.73M repos 2024 (Cox).
Cards ~$1.25T; BNPL ~$122B GMV / ~$400B invisible / 47% late; student ~$1.66T, 90+ ~7.74% (Q1 2025).
Total ABS ~$358B 2025 (+16.8%); auto ABS ~$127B; banks warehouse then securitize off-balance-sheet.
